tool_usage_for_slash_commands.overview: Specific guidance on using tools effectively when creating and managing slash commands. This mode has restricted file access to command files only.

restriction.description: This mode can only edit slash command markdown files
restriction.note: Attempting to edit other files will result in FileRestrictionError

priority.purpose: Explore and understand existing commands before creating new ones
priority.rationale: Prevents duplicate commands and ensures consistency
Make targeted updates to command files

priority.purpose: Create directories or perform file operations
priority.rationale: Sometimes needed to set up command directories

tool.name: list_files
tool.purpose: Explore command directories and check for existing commands

practice.action: Verify directory exists before creating files

example.description: Before creating a new command, check what exists
example.interpretation: - Look for similar command names - Check naming patterns used - Identify possible duplicates

tool.name: read_file
tool.purpose: Examine existing commands to understand patterns and avoid duplicates

practice.reason: Match the tone and structure of existing commands

example.description: Read an existing command to understand the pattern
example.what_to_look_for: - Frontmatter fields used - Content structure (lists, sections) - Level of detail - Project-specific references

practice.reason: Required for Roo Code to recognize as command

practice.action: Use lowercase with hyphens in filenames
practice.reason: Follows naming conventions and creates clean command names

practice.action: Include frontmatter for all non-trivial commands
practice.reason: Provides better UX in command menu

example.description: Create a basic command without arguments
example.code:
write_to_file( path=".roo/commands/optimize.md", content="""--- description: Analyze code for performance optimizations ---
Analyze the selected code for performance improvements: - Identify inefficient algorithms - Find unnecessary computations - Suggest caching opportunities - Recommend async improvements - Check for memory leaks """ )

example.description: Create a command that expects arguments
example.code:
write_to_file( path=".roo/commands/create-component.md", content="""--- description: Create a new React component with TypeScript argument-hint: <component-name> mode: code ---
Create a new React component with the specified name: - Use TypeScript with proper prop types - Include JSDoc comments - Add CSS module for styling - Create Storybook story - Write unit tests with React Testing Library
Follow our project's component structure and naming conventions. """ )

example.description: Create a command that runs in specific mode
example.code:
write_to_file( path=".roo/commands/architecture-review.md", content="""--- description: Analyze and improve system architecture mode: architect ---
Review the system architecture and provide recommendations:
**Analysis:** 1. Document component hierarchy and relationships 2. Identify coupling and cohesion issues 3. Review data flow patterns 4. Assess scalability concerns
**Recommendations:** - Propose architectural improvements - Suggest refactoring strategies - Document design patterns to adopt - Create architecture diagrams """ )

when_to_use.case: Adding new requirements to existing command

practice.reason: Ensure exact content match for SEARCH block
Required for apply_diff to work correctly
Easier to review and less error-prone

example.description: Add argument hint to existing command
example.prerequisite: Read the file first to get exact content
example.code: apply_diff( path=".roo/commands/api-endpoint.md", diff=""" <<<<<<< SEARCH :start_line:1 ------- --- description: Generate REST API endpoint with best practices --- ======= --- description: Generate REST API endpoint with best practices argument-hint: <endpoint-name> <http-method> mode: code --- >>>>>>> REPLACE """ )

apply_diff( path=".roo/commands/review.md", diff=""" <<<<<<< SEARCH :start_line:5 ------- Review the code for issues: - Check for bugs - Look at performance ======= Review the code for potential issues: - Identify logical bugs and edge cases - Analyze performance bottlenecks - Check for security vulnerabilities - Verify error handling >>>>>>> REPLACE """ )

tool.name: execute_command
tool.purpose: Create directories or perform file system operations

when_to_use.case: Need to create subdirectories for organization
Want to list commands using shell commands

practice.action: Use mkdir -p for safe directory creation
practice.reason: Creates parent directories and doesn't error if exists

practice.action: Check directory existence with list_files first

example.description: Create the commands directory if it doesn't exist
example.note: Use -p flag to avoid errors if directory exists
Subdirectories are supported for command organization
Global commands are stored in home directory

when_to_use.case: Need to determine scope (global vs project)
Unclear what arguments the command should accept

practice.action: Order suggestions by likelihood or best practice
practice.reason: User can quickly select the most common option

example.question: Should this be a global or project-specific command?

suggestions.suggestion: Global (~/.roo/commands/) - just for me across all projects

example.question: What arguments should this command accept?

suggestions.suggestion: No arguments - work with selected code

example.question: Should this command target a specific mode?

suggestions.suggestion: No mode - works in any mode

sequence.name: create_new_command
sequence.description: Complete workflow for creating a new command

step.purpose: Check existing commands to avoid duplicates
Read similar commands for reference (if any exist)
Create directory if it doesn't exist

sequence.name: update_existing_command
sequence.description: Workflow for modifying an existing command

sequence.name: migrate_to_project
sequence.description: Move global command to project scope
sequence.note: User must manually delete global version if desired

error.solution: Only edit files matching the allowed patterns
Use execute_command to create it: mkdir -p .roo/commands
- Check for proper --- delimiters - Verify YAML syntax (colons, indentation) - Ensure quotes around strings with special chars

error.description: Command file created but doesn't show in menu
error.troubleshooting: - Verify .md extension is present - Check file is in correct directory - Ensure filename uses valid characters - Try reloading VS Code window
